name: Agent: Web Development
summary: Turns a Figma design into a real page that matches it — fast, accessible, and a proper page in every language it ships in.
objectives: Turn a Figma design into a real page that matches it — and prove the match, instead of eyeballing it.
A page that misses the quality bar does not ship. The gate decides, not an opinion.
Every language is its own real page that Google can find — not text swapped in by a script.

outcomes.text: It built a landing page from one design frame and put it live in two languages. Each language is a real page that points at the other, so a search engine finds both.
The page scores 97, 97, 100 and 100 on Google's own quality test — speed, accessibility, best practice, SEO. The bar to ship is 90 on all four.
Nobody types SEO into the code. It is generated from the project's own data, and a console lets someone who does not code review and fix it before launch.
Each language gets its own preview card for when someone shares the link — in that language. The picture has words on it, so the words get translated too.

Matching the design is measured three ways instead of one. A single "percent different" score never reaches zero once there are photographs on the page — and a bar nobody can clear is a bar everybody ignores.

milestones.title: Write the rules: layout, screen sizes, dark mode, SEO, languages and the quality gate
Make an approved brief a hard gate — no build starts without one
Turn the quality checks into code that can stop a delivery
Build the first landing page from a real design
Ship it in two languages, each a real page
Generate the SEO instead of hand-writing it, with a console for people who do not code
Feed what the first project taught back into the rules
Set the agent up in the shared workspace, so anyone can start a project with it
